title: Did You Know? Nigeria Has the Largest Economy in Africa
summary: Nigeria's GDP surpassed South Africa's in 2014 after a rebasing exercise, making it the largest economy on the African continent.
content:
Nigeria's economy became the largest in Africa in 2014 after the National Bureau of Statistics conducted a GDP rebasing exercise that added previously uncounted sectors.

The rebased GDP stood at approximately $510 billion, surpassing South Africa's $370 billion at the time.

Key sectors driving Nigeria's economy include oil and gas, telecommunications, agriculture, and entertainment (Nollywood is the world's second-largest film industry by volume).

Despite its size, Nigeria faces significant challenges including infrastructure deficits, youth unemployment, and income inequality â issues that Big Quams Media works to help Nigerian students navigate through education and awareness.

title: Today in History: Nigeria Gained Independence on October 1, 1960
summary: On October 1, 1960, Nigeria officially became an independent nation, ending over 60 years of British colonial rule.
content:
On October 1, 1960, the green-white-green flag was hoisted for the first time as Nigeria became an independent sovereign nation.

Sir Abubakar Tafawa Balewa became the first Prime Minister of independent Nigeria, while Dr. Nnamdi Azikiwe later became the first President.

The independence was achieved through peaceful negotiations with the British government, unlike many African nations that had to fight for liberation.

Today, Nigeria celebrates October 1st as Independence Day â a public holiday that commemorates this historic achievement and reflects on the nation's journey since 1960.

Nigeria has since grown to become a nation of over 220 million people, the most populous country in Africa.

title: Current Affairs: JAMB Introduces New CBT Guidelines for 2026
summary: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board has released updated guidelines for the 2026 UTME, including new biometric requirements and profile code generation steps.
content:
The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has issued comprehensive new guidelines for the 2026 Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination.

Key updates include:
- Mandatory biometric verification at accredited CBT centres
- NIN must be linked to an active phone number before registration
- Profile codes must be generated before proceeding to any centre
- Payment of the official registration fee of â¦3,700 only through approved channels

JAMB has also warned against patronising unauthorised registration agents who charge above the official fees.

Candidates are advised to start their registration process early to avoid last-minute complications. Big Quams Media provides step-by-step registration support â contact us on WhatsApp for personalised guidance.
